title: RepoBrief agent benchmark against grep/read
goal: Measure the incremental value of RepoBrief MCP for real coding-agent work against the ordinary grep, glob and targeted-read workflow without fabricating model runs or promoting RepoBrief by default.
completion: A frozen multi-repository taskset, provider-neutral runner contract and deterministic evaluator are implemented and validated.
A read-only provider runner is separately qualified for exact model identity, provider-reported usage, tool-call capture, isolated checkouts and immutable transcripts.

A separate live preflight proves one hard-cost-bounded baseline/treatment pair, treatment MCP availability, valid receipts, source integrity and separated snapshot/freshness latency before batch dispatch.
Real paired baseline and RepoBrief-augmented agent runs use the same model, prompt, budgets and isolated sessions with provider-reported tokens and bound transcripts.
Navigation, structural reasoning, grounding, freshness, false confidence, time, tool-call and token outcomes are reported without post-hoc task tuning.
RepoBrief remains opt-in unless a separate reviewed decision establishes a reproducible benefit without material quality or safety regression.

metadata.comparison: baseline grep/glob/read versus the same tools plus RepoBrief MCP

RAB-V1-T001.outcome: Frozen taskset, provider-neutral runner boundary, complete pair matrix, receipt integrity checks and deterministic evaluator are merged and validated.

RAB-V1-T002A.outcome: The isolated read-only Claude runner is merged with frozen-plan binding, derived identities, strict tool boundaries, hash-bound transcripts, provider evidence validation and synthetic-only fixture reports.

RAB-V1-T002B.outcome: The live preflight was not a valid pair: baseline was executed twice, three provider processes were observed and treatment ended with error_max_budget_usd at USD 0.1358768 under a USD 0.12 ceiling. No retry is permitted.

RAB-V1-T002.condition: Blocked until T002H is separately authorized, executed once and verified. T002G is verified and does not itself authorize provider use.

RAB-V1-T003.condition: Requires completed real paired runs from RAB-V1-T002.

RAB-V1-T002C.outcome: One-shot integrated preflight merged and synthetically qualified; no live provider use.

RAB-V1-T002D.outcome: The separately authorized one-shot attempt was consumed but failed locally before provider dispatch because argparse option abbreviation removed the absolute --claude-command argument. No live output tree, provider intent or cost was created; no retry is permitted.

RAB-V1-T002E.outcome: Exact adapter, planner and export contracts were hardened and verified without provider use; Grabowski PR #195 and Lenskit PR #1002 are merged.

RAB-V1-T002F.outcome: The fresh r2 one-shot launch failed before any condition or provider intent because the installed 259,820,344-byte Claude executable exceeded the obsolete 64 MiB identity limit. Provider intents: 0; cost: USD 0; retry prohibited.

RAB-V1-T002G.outcome: Claude executable identity hardening is merged in Grabowski PR #259. Historical provider-free proof covered Claude 2.1.208 at 259,820,344 bytes; current closeout revalidated installed Claude 2.1.217 at 268,573,680 bytes. No provider process or cost was produced.

RAB-V1-T002H.condition: Requires verified T002G, fresh state, separate review and no known provider/session-limit blockade.

metadata.sequencing: Keep failed T002B, consumed invalid T002D and consumed pre-dispatch T002F visible. T002G is verified provider-free. T002H remains planned and requires fresh state, separate review and a new explicit live authorization before exactly one new pair may be attempted. Only after a valid verified pair may the real paired benchmark proceed.
does_not_establish: answer correctness outside the fixed taskset
metadata.lifecycle_reason: T002G is verified provider-free; remaining benchmark path is intentionally waiting on separately authorized T002H before T002 can proceed.
